2606.23336.title: WaveDetect: Robust Framework for Machine-Generated Text Detection via Wavelet Transform
2606.23336.paper_summary: As Large Language Models asymptotically approach human-level fluency in natural language generation, solely relying on surface-level semantic artifacts for detecting LLM-generated texts has become increasingly precarious. Existing detectors often falter when facing three critical challenges: adversarial perturbations, cross-domain shifts, and the rapid temporal evolution of the foundation model. To address these issues, we propose \wavedetect, a novel framework that reformulates text detection as a signal processing task within the time-frequency domain. Unlike previous methods that analyze static token probability distributions, \wavedetect models the generated output as a probability signal, upon which a differentiable Continuous Wavelet Transform is applied to convert them into learnable spectral representations. This process reveals the intrinsic ``spectral fingerprints'' in machine-generated texts--patterns that remain invisible in time domain. Comprehensive evaluations on three well-curated datasets (RAID, EvoBench, and Domain-Shift) show that our method achieves a new state-of-the-art. It not only achieves superior accuracy but also exhibits remarkable robustness against sophisticated attacks, generalization across out-of-distribution topics and unseen evolving LLMs. Our results validate the efficacy of spectral analysis as a promising paradigm for LLM-generated texts detection.
2606.23336.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型在自然語言生成方面逐漸接近人類水平，僅依賴表面語義特徵來檢測LLM生成的文本變得越來越危險。現有的檢測器在面對三個關鍵挑戰時往往會失效：對抗性擾動、跨領域轉移以及基礎模型的快速時間演變。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了\wavedetect，一個將文本檢測重新定義為時間-頻率域內信號處理任務的新框架。與以往分析靜態標記概率分佈的方法不同，\wavedetect將生成的輸出建模為概率信號，並在其上應用可微分的連續小波變換，將其轉換為可學習的頻譜表示。這個過程揭示了機器生成文本中的內在“頻譜指紋”——在時間域中無法看見的模式。在三個精心策劃的數據集（RAID、EvoBench和Domain-Shift）上的全面評估顯示，我們的方法達到了新的最先進水平。它不僅實現了卓越的準確性，還在對抗複雜攻擊、跨分佈主題的泛化以及未見的演變LLM方面展現了顯著的穩健性。我們的結果驗證了頻譜分析作為檢測LLM生成文本的有前景範式的有效性。
2606.23336.author: Zhichen Liu et.al.
2606.23336.authors: Zhichen Liu, Kaitong Qin, Linhan He, Yang Xu
